Read `customers.csv` with columns `customer_id,name,email,signup_date`. Trim whitespace from all string fields, lowercase `email`, parse `signup_date` as `YYYY-MM-DD`, validate that `customer_id` is unique and every email contains exactly one `@`, then write newline-delimited JSON to `customers.jsonl`.

Read `orders.json` containing an array of objects with `order_id`, `customer_id`, `amount`, and `currency`. Convert `amount` to a decimal with 2 places, validate that `order_id` is unique and `currency` is one of `USD`, `EUR`, or `GBP`, then output `orders.csv` sorted by `order_id`.

Read all `*.log` files in `input/logs/`, where each line is `timestamp|service|status_code|latency_ms`. Parse each line, keep only records with `status_code >= 500`, validate timestamps are valid ISO 8601 datetimes, then write a single Parquet file `failed_requests.parquet`.

Read `products.xml` containing `<product>` elements with `sku`, `name`, `price`, and `in_stock`. Convert it to JSON, cast `price` to float and `in_stock` to boolean, validate that every `sku` matches `^[A-Z0-9_-]+$`, then write `products.json`.

Read `transactions.xlsx` from sheet `Sheet1` with columns `txn_id`, `account_id`, `posted_at`, `debit`, `credit`. Transform by adding `net = credit - debit`, validate no row has both `debit > 0` and `credit > 0`, then output `transactions_with_net.csv`.

Read `events.jsonl`, where each line is a JSON object with `event_id`, `user_id`, `type`, and `ts`. Convert `ts` from Unix seconds to ISO 8601 UTC, validate `event_id` is unique across the file, then write Avro output to `events.avro`.

Fetch paginated data from `GET https://api.example.com/v1/users?page=N` until an empty `data` array is returned. For each user, keep `id`, `full_name`, and `created_at`, rename `full_name` to `name`, validate all `id` values are unique across pages, then write `users.csv`.

Read `inventory.csv` and `suppliers.csv`. Join on `supplier_id`, compute `inventory_value = quantity * unit_cost`, validate every inventory row matches a supplier, then write `inventory_enriched.json`.

Read `temperature_readings.tsv` with columns `sensor_id`, `recorded_at`, `temp_f`. Convert `temp_f` to Celsius rounded to 1 decimal place, validate `temp_f` is between `-100` and `200`, then output `temperature_readings_celsius.csv`.

Read `support_tickets.yaml`, where each ticket has `ticket_id`, `priority`, `opened_at`, and `tags`. Normalize `priority` to uppercase, sort `tags` alphabetically, validate `priority` is one of `LOW`, `MEDIUM`, `HIGH`, `URGENT`, then write `support_tickets.jsonl`.

Read `payments.csv` with `payment_id`, `customer_id`, `amount`, `paid_at`. Fetch exchange rates once from `GET https://api.example.com/v1/fx/latest?base=USD`, convert all amounts from USD to EUR using the returned EUR rate, validate the API response contains a numeric `rates.EUR`, then write `payments_eur.csv`.

Read `employees.json` containing nested objects with `id`, `profile.name`, `profile.department`, and `salary`. Flatten the nested structure, validate `salary` is a non-negative integer, then output `employees_flat.csv`.
Read `visits.csv` with `visit_id`, `user_id`, `page`, `duration_seconds`. Group by `user_id` and sum `duration_seconds`, validate that no duration is negative, then write `user_visit_totals.json`.

Read a gzip-compressed file `clickstream.jsonl.gz`. Decompress, parse each JSON line, keep only records where `event_type = "purchase"`, validate `timestamp` exists on every retained record, then write `purchases.csv`.

Read `bank_codes_fixed_width.txt` where positions `1-4` = `bank_code`, `5-24` = `bank_name`, `25-26` = `country`. Parse into structured records, trim text fields, validate `country` is exactly 2 uppercase letters, then output `bank_codes.json`.
Read `movies.csv` with `movie_id`, `title`, `genres`. Split `genres` on `|` into arrays, validate no movie has an empty `title`, then write `movies.jsonl`.

Fetch `GET https://api.example.com/v1/weather/cities`, which returns JSON objects with `city`, `temp_k`, and `humidity`. Convert `temp_k` to Celsius, validate `humidity` is between `0` and `100`, then output `weather.xml`.

Read `sales_2025_q1.csv`, `sales_2025_q2.csv`, `sales_2025_q3.csv`, and `sales_2025_q4.csv`. Concatenate them, validate the resulting dataset has no duplicate `sale_id`, then write `sales_2025.parquet`.

Read `accounts.jsonl` with `account_id`, `opened_date`, `status`. Parse `opened_date` from `MM/DD/YYYY` to ISO `YYYY-MM-DD`, uppercase `status`, validate `status` is one of `ACTIVE`, `SUSPENDED`, `CLOSED`, then output `accounts.csv`.

Read `sensor_packets.bin`, where each record is 16 bytes: 4-byte unsigned int `device_id`, 8-byte signed int Unix milliseconds `ts`, 4-byte float `reading`, little-endian. Parse records, convert timestamps to ISO 8601 UTC, validate file length is a multiple of 16, then write `sensor_packets.json`.
Read `articles.xml`, extract `id`, `title`, and HTML body content from each article. Strip HTML tags from body, validate `id` is present for every article, then output `articles.csv`.

Read `geo_points.json` with fields `id`, `latitude`, `longitude`. Validate latitude is in `[-90, 90]` and longitude is in `[-180, 180]`, then convert to GeoJSON FeatureCollection in `geo_points.geojson`.

Fetch `GET https://api.example.com/v1/orders?updated_since=2025-01-01T00:00:00Z`. For each order, extract `id`, `status`, `updated_at`, validate `updated_at` is on or after `2025-01-01T00:00:00Z`, then write newline-delimited JSON to `orders_incremental.jsonl`.
Read `emails.mbox`, parse messages, extract `Message-ID`, `From`, `Date`, and plain-text body length, validate every message has a `Message-ID`, then output `emails_summary.csv`.
Read `students.csv` with `student_id`, `name`, `score_math`, `score_science`. Add `average_score`, validate both scores are between `0` and `100`, then write `students_with_average.xlsx`.

Read `iot_readings.proto.bin`, where each record is a protobuf-encoded `Reading { string device_id = 1; int64 ts = 2; double value = 3; }` delimited by varint length prefixes. Decode each record, convert `ts` from Unix milliseconds to ISO 8601 UTC, validate `device_id` is non-empty, then write `iot_readings.jsonl`.

Read `returns.csv` and `orders.csv`. Left join returns onto orders by `order_id`, add boolean field `was_returned`, validate every return references an existing order, then output `orders_with_returns.parquet`.

Read `webhooks.ndjson`, where each line has `event`, `payload`, and `received_at`. Expand nested `payload.user.id` into top-level `user_id`, validate `received_at` is valid ISO 8601, then write `webhooks_flat.csv`.

Fetch `GET https://api.example.com/v1/products` and `GET https://api.example.com/v1/categories`. Join products to categories using `category_id`, replace `category_id` with `category_name`, validate every product has a matching category, then write `products_enriched.yaml`.

Read `audit.csv` with `actor`, `action`, `resource`, `occurred_at`. Create a new field `event_key = actor + ":" + action + ":" + resource`, validate no field used in the key is empty, then output sorted JSON by `occurred_at` ascending to `audit.json`.

Read `meter_readings.csv` with `meter_id`, `read_date`, `reading`. For each `meter_id`, sort by `read_date` and compute day-over-day difference from the previous reading, validate readings never decrease within a meter, then write `meter_deltas.csv`.

Read `forum_posts.jsonl`, detect and remove duplicate posts by identical `post_id`, normalize line endings in `content` to `\n`, validate `author_id` exists on every retained post, then write `forum_posts_deduped.jsonl`.

Read `exchange_rates.xml` with rates relative to EUR and `invoices.csv` with amounts in EUR. Parse the XML, convert all invoice amounts to GBP using the GBP rate, validate the XML contains exactly one GBP rate, then output `invoices_gbp.csv`.

Read `recipes.yaml`, where each recipe has `recipe_id`, `ingredients`, and `prep_minutes`. Explode each ingredient into a separate row with `recipe_id`, validate `prep_minutes` is a positive integer, then write `recipe_ingredients.csv`.

Read `device_statuses.csv` with `device_id`, `status`, `last_seen`. Keep only the latest row per `device_id` by `last_seen`, validate `last_seen` parses as ISO 8601, then output `latest_device_status.json`.

Fetch `GET https://api.example.com/v1/shipments?page=N` with cursor-based pagination using response field `next_cursor`. Continue until `next_cursor` is null. Flatten each shipment to `shipment_id`, `carrier`, `eta`, validate `shipment_id` is unique across all pages, then write `shipments.csv`.
Read `library_books.csv` with `isbn`, `title`, `published_year`. Normalize `isbn` by removing hyphens, validate normalized ISBN length is either 10 or 13 digits, then output `library_books.json`.

Read `app_metrics.json` containing an array of `{service, cpu, memory_mb}`. Bucket `cpu` into `low` (`<40`), `medium` (`40-79.999`), `high` (`>=80`), validate `memory_mb` is a non-negative number, then write `app_metrics.csv`.

Read `images_manifest.csv` with `image_id`, `url`, and `label`. Fetch each image URL with an HTTP GET and record the response status code and byte size, validate every URL starts with `https://`, then write `images_fetch_report.jsonl`.

Read `claims.csv` with `claim_id`, `member_id`, `submitted_at`, `amount`. Parse `submitted_at`, add `submission_month` as `YYYY-MM`, validate `amount >= 0`, then write `claims_partitioned/` as one CSV file per `submission_month`.

Read `music_tracks.xml`, extract `track_id`, `artist`, `duration_seconds`. Convert `duration_seconds` to `duration_minutes` rounded to 2 decimals, validate duration is greater than 0, then write `music_tracks.jsonl`.
Read `dns_records.zone`, parse `A` records only into `name`, `ttl`, `ip`, validate each `ip` is a valid IPv4 address, then output `dns_a_records.csv`.

Fetch `GET https://api.example.com/v1/customers` and write only customers created in the last 30 days relative to `2025-03-01T00:00:00Z`. Normalize phone numbers by removing spaces and dashes, validate the resulting phone number contains only digits and optional leading `+`, then output `recent_customers.json`.

Read `retail.ndjson`, where each record has `store_id`, `sku`, `units`, `unit_price`. Compute `revenue = units * unit_price`, validate `units` is an integer and `unit_price >= 0`, then write aggregated total revenue per `store_id` to `store_revenue.csv`.
Read `clinical_trials.csv` with `trial_id`, `phase`, `start_date`, `end_date`. Parse dates, compute `duration_days`, validate `end_date >= start_date`, then write `clinical_trials.json`.

Read `chat_export.txt`, where messages are delimited by blank lines and each message begins with `[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] username:`. Parse messages, extract timestamp, username, and message text, validate every block matches the expected header pattern, then output `chat_export.csv`.
Read `energy_usage.xlsx` with sheets `January`, `February`, and `March`, all sharing columns `site_id`, `date`, `kwh`. Stack all sheets, validate `kwh >= 0`, then write `energy_usage_q1.jsonl`.

Read `vendors.json`, then for each vendor call `GET https://api.example.com/v1/risk/{vendor_id}`. Merge the returned `risk_score` into each vendor record, validate `risk_score` is an integer from 1 to 100, then write `vendors_with_risk.csv`.

Read `markdown_posts/` containing `*.md` files with YAML front matter fields `title`, `author`, and `date`. Parse front matter and body separately, compute `word_count` from the Markdown body after removing formatting markers, validate required front matter fields are present, then write `posts_index.json`.

Read `orders.csv`, convert it to JSON, then fetch tax rates from `GET https://api.example.com/v1/tax?country={country}` for each distinct country in the data. Add `tax_rate` and `tax_amount = amount * tax_rate`, validate every API response contains a numeric rate between `0` and `1`, then write the final result as `orders_taxed.xml`.
